title: cornus stolonifera var. interior
intro: Cornus stolonifera var. interior, also known as Bitter American Dogwood, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, characterized by its red or yellowish stems and clusters of small white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiviral compounds, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cornus stolonifera var. interior include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit immunomodulatory effects, making it a sought-after herb for treating various ailments. Herbal preparations of Cornus stolonifera var. interior include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and skin irritations.
